message:
fix(config): align default projects directory with README documentation

Change default projects directory from $HOME/projects to /data/projects
to match the documented behavior in README.md.

Changes in ru:
- Line 178: DEFAULT_PROJECTS_DIR="${RU_PROJECTS_DIR:-/data/projects}"
- Line 358: repo_spec_to_path() projects_dir default

Problem (bd-13z0):
The README documentation shows examples using /data/projects as the
default location where repositories are cloned. However, the actual
code defaulted to $HOME/projects, causing confusion when users followed
the documentation but found repos in a different location.

Example from README:
  $ ru sync
  Cloning: owner/repo -> /data/projects/repo

But actual behavior was:
  $ ru sync
  Cloning: owner/repo -> /home/user/projects/repo

Solution:
Align the code defaults with the documentation. Users who prefer
$HOME/projects can still set RU_PROJECTS_DIR environment variable
or use `ru config --set projects_dir=$HOME/projects`.

Impact:
- New installations will use /data/projects by default
- Existing users with RU_PROJECTS_DIR set are unaffected
- Users who relied on implicit $HOME/projects default should set
  RU_PROJECTS_DIR explicitly or update their workflow

Migration:
  # To keep old behavior:
  export RU_PROJECTS_DIR="$HOME/projects"
  # Or:
  ru config --set projects_dir="$HOME/projects"

Fixes: bd-13z0

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -175,7 +175,7 @@ fi
 RU_LOG_DIR="$RU_STATE_DIR/logs"
 
 # Default configuration values
-DEFAULT_PROJECTS_DIR="${RU_PROJECTS_DIR:-$HOME/projects}"
+DEFAULT_PROJECTS_DIR="${RU_PROJECTS_DIR:-/data/projects}"
 DEFAULT_LAYOUT="flat"           # flat | owner-repo | full
 DEFAULT_UPDATE_STRATEGY="ff-only"  # ff-only | rebase | merge
 DEFAULT_AUTOSTASH="false"
@@ -355,7 +355,7 @@ repo_spec_to_path() {
     # Extract repo name (last component)
     local repo_name="${repo##*/}"
     # Use configured projects directory
-    local projects_dir="${RU_PROJECTS_DIR:-$HOME/projects}"
+    local projects_dir="${RU_PROJECTS_DIR:-/data/projects}"
 
     echo "${projects_dir}/${repo_name}"
 }
